name: Asymmetric signing & JWK export
outcome: JwtService can sign and verify tokens with a consumer-supplied asymmetric keypair and expose the public key as a JWK set, while the existing symmetric path is untouched.
scope: Extend JwtService for asymmetric (RS256/ES256) keys, fix sign() to forward SignOptions, and add an optional JWKS-exposing method to JwtInterface. No endpoints, no OIDC config yet.
acceptance_criteria: A JwtService built with an RSA or EC private key signs and verifies RS256/ES256 tokens, and the JWT header includes a kid
getKeySet returns a valid JWK set containing the public key with a kid matching the token header
kid defaults to the RFC 7638 thumbprint and can be overridden
The existing string-secret HS256 path signs and verifies byte-identically to today
steps: JwtService constructor — accept an asymmetric key option object ({ key, kid?, algorithm? }) alongside the existing Secret string, deriving the public key from the private key via Node crypto
JwtService.sign — forward SignOptions (algorithm, keyid) to jsonwebtoken so RS256/ES256 tokens carry a kid header

JwtService — auto-detect algorithm from key type (RSA to RS256, EC to ES256) and default kid to the RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint (a hand-rolled function: canonical JSON of the required JWK members, SHA-256, base64url — Node crypto has no thumbprint primitive)
JwtInterface — add an optional getKeySet method exposing the public key as a JWK set, and implement it on JwtService
test/unit — cover RS256/ES256 round trips, JWK export, thumbprint kid, and confirm the symmetric HS256 path is unchanged
notes: Foundation slice — unblocks everything. JwtService.sign currently drops its options argument; fixing that is required here. No new dependency: jsonwebtoken plus Node crypto (createPublicKey, JWK export). The RFC 7638 thumbprint has no crypto primitive — it is a small hand-rolled function and should be unit-tested directly. See docs/adr/0001.
